cases.name: navigate to url
cases.task: Open https://example.com in the browser.

cases.name: inspect page for actionable refs
cases.task: Inspect the current page snapshot target page for actionable refs.

cases.name: capture full page visual proof
cases.task: Take a full-page screenshot of the current page for visual proof.

cases.name: click known target
cases.task: Click the Submit button using target ref submit-button from the current snapshot.

cases.name: type search and submit
cases.task: Type "playwright mcp" into the search input target search-box and press Enter.

cases.name: fill multi field form
cases.task: Fill the multi field form with email user@example.com and name Ada Lovelace.

cases.name: select dropdown option
cases.task: Select Canada from the country dropdown target country-select.

cases.name: wait for success text
cases.task: Wait until the text Success appears on the page.

cases.name: evaluate local storage
cases.task: Read window.localStorage.getItem('authToken') from the current page.

cases.name: press escape key
cases.task: Press Escape to close the current modal.

cases.name: list api network requests
cases.task: List network requests since page load, filtered to URLs matching /api/.

cases.name: inspect numbered network request
cases.task: Show full details for network request #3 from the previous request list.

cases.name: get console errors
cases.task: Return browser console errors since the last navigation.

cases.name: discover schemas
cases.task: I just connected this Postgres MCP server. What schemas are available?

cases.name: list schema objects
cases.task: Show me the tables, views, sequences, and extensions inside the analytics schema.

cases.name: inspect table structure
cases.task: Before writing SQL, inspect public.orders so we know its columns, constraints, and indexes.

cases.name: run ready read query
cases.task: Run this ready query now: SELECT count(*) FROM public.orders WHERE status = 'failed';

cases.name: get query plan
cases.task: Do not run the query for rows. Show the PostgreSQL execution plan and planner cost for SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 42;

cases.name: rank slow workload queries
cases.task: Rank the top 5 slow workload queries by total execution time.

cases.name: workload index tuning
cases.task: Analyze workload indexes with at most 3 candidate indexes.

cases.name: specific query index tuning
cases.task: Suggest the best indexes for this specific SQL: SELECT * FROM orders JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id WHERE orders.created_at > '2026-01-01';

cases.name: database health check
cases.task: Run a database-wide operational health review covering connections, vacuum, invalid constraints, duplicate or unused indexes, sequences, and buffer cache.

cases.name: list channels to find id
cases.task: List up to 20 Slack channels so I can find the right channel id.

cases.name: post top level channel message
cases.task: Post a new top-level message to channel C123: Deploy is complete.

cases.name: reply in existing thread
cases.task: Reply in channel C123 under thread 1712345678.000100 with: I will investigate.

cases.name: read recent channel messages
cases.task: Get recent messages from channel C123.

cases.name: read thread replies
cases.task: Get all replies in channel C123 under thread 1712345678.000100.

cases.name: add reaction to message
cases.task: Add the :eyes: reaction to message 1712345678.000100 in channel C123.

cases.name: list users to find id
cases.task: List up to 20 Slack users so I can find the right user id.

cases.name: get known user profile
cases.task: Get detailed profile information for Slack user U123.

cases.name: list public tables
cases.task: For Supabase project proj_abc, list the public schema tables.

cases.name: inspect table columns
cases.task: For project proj_abc, show the columns, primary keys, and foreign keys for tables in the public schema.

cases.name: list applied migrations
cases.task: For project proj_abc, list the migrations that have already been applied.

cases.name: list installed extensions
cases.task: For project proj_abc, list the installed Postgres extensions.

cases.name: run read only report query
cases.task: For project proj_abc, run this SQL report: SELECT status, count(*) FROM orders GROUP BY status;

cases.name: ddl create table uses migration
cases.task: For project proj_abc, run this SQL: CREATE TABLE public.audit_events (id bigint generated always as identity primary key, created_at timestamptz default now());

cases.name: ddl alter table uses migration
cases.task: For project proj_abc, apply this schema change: ALTER TABLE public.orders ADD COLUMN archived_at timestamptz;

cases.name: ddl create index uses migration
cases.task: For project proj_abc, run this SQL to speed up lookups: CREATE INDEX orders_customer_id_idx ON public.orders(customer_id);

cases.name: rls policy uses migration
cases.task: For project proj_abc, add an RLS policy so users can select their own rows in public.profiles.

cases.name: unknown relation error inspects schema
cases.task: For Supabase project proj_abc, the previous execute_sql call failed with: relation "public.orders_2026" does not exist. Before trying another query, inspect the public schema tables to find the correct table name.

cases.name: table inventory concise skips verbose
cases.task: For Supabase project proj_abc, list only the public table names. Keep the result concise and do not include columns, primary keys, or foreign key details.

cases.name: schema metadata before select star
cases.task: For project proj_abc, I need to know which columns exist before writing a SELECT. Inspect the public schema metadata first instead of running SELECT *.

cases.name: full trace error recovers to discovery
cases.task: The previous Zymtrace hot_traces call failed because meta_only=false was requested without a selected prefix_hash. Recover by doing first-pass CPU hot trace discovery for the default project between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

cases.name: hot trace discovery is bounded
